commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review remains appropriate because the patch changes provider/model override authorization and no narrow automated repair is identified.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found; the internal model override flag is authorization-sensitive but bounded to existing gateway request fields and tracked as merge risk. (sha=34c009def10cf997dbf5cacb0daf85b16b59860a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards explicit `sessions_spawn.model` selections into child gateway `agent` requests, authorizes the internal override path, and adds regression tests for explicit and default sub-agent spawns.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +85. Total +113 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main resolves and persists the requested sub-agent model, but the child gateway `agent` request omits provider/model. I did not run the exact QQ/Qwen/DeepSeek scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review remains appropriate because the patch changes provider/model override authorization and no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: fix #91171: [Bug]: Sub-agent model routing ignores model parameter, silently falls back to deepseek
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `sessions_spawn` could report an explicit requested model as applied while the child `agent` gateway launch still omitted provider/model and ran through the default route.
- Solution: forward the resolved provider/model into the child launch only for explicit `sessions_spawn.model`, and authorize that override at the existing gateway boundary.
- What changed: `spawnSubagentDirect` now adds provider/model to the initial child `agent` request for explicit model overrides; in-process gateway dispatch can merge internal `allowModelOverride` into the scoped client so plugin/run metadata is preserved.
- What did NOT change: no new config/schema/protocol fields; no model catalog/default policy change; no-explicit-model sub-agent spawns do not forward provider/model as explicit gateway overrides.
- Why it matters / User impact: users spawning a sub-agent for a model-specific task, such as image-capable Qwen from a default DeepSeek session, need the actual child run to use that requested model instead of silently falling back to a model without the needed capabilities.
- Fixes #91171

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Explicit `sessions_spawn.model` was resolved and reported but not passed to the child gateway `agent` request that starts the actual sub-agent run.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Windows Git Bash, using Node 22.19.0 from npm `node@22.19.0`, an isolated loopback OpenClaw gateway, and a configured Claude CLI backend for `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6`; focused Vitest used the repository `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` wrapper.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Live proof: started a local loopback gateway with isolated config/state and invoked `spawnSubagentDirect({ model: "claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6", mode: "run", cleanup: "keep" })` against that gateway; then read gateway `status` for the spawned child session. Regression: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`; `git diff --check`.
- **Evidence after fix:** The live spawn returned `status: "accepted"`, `resolvedProvider: "claude-cli"`, `resolvedModel: "claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6"`, and `modelApplied: true` for child session `agent:dev:subagent:086240a8-3915-42ab-97e4-41b84c29704e`; a follow-up gateway `status` row for that child showed `kind: "spawn-child"`, `configuredModel: "claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6"`, `selectedModel: "claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6"`, and `runtime: "Claude CLI"`. The focused Vitest command exited 0, and `git diff --check` exited 0.
- **Observed result after fix:** A real local spawned child run selected the same explicit provider/model that `sessions_spawn`/`spawnSubagentDirect` resolved, instead of falling back to the default route. Tests additionally assert that explicit `sessions_spawn.model: "openai/gpt-5.4"` produces child `agent` params with `provider: "openai"` and `model: "gpt-5.4"`; in-process explicit `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5` dispatch forwards provider/model with internal override authorization; no-explicit-model spawn asserts provider/model remain undefined.
- **What was not tested:** The reporter's exact live QQ/Qwen/DeepSeek channel setup was not run. The live proof uses a local Claude CLI provider/model available in this environment, exercising the same gateway launch boundary identified in the issue: whether the requested provider/model is selected by the spawned child run.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Addressed with redacted local live gateway proof: explicit `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` spawn returned `modelApplied: true`, and the spawned child status row selected `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` on `Claude CLI`.
- **RF-002:** Addressed by adding the live proof fields to `Real behavior proof`; exact QQ/Qwen/DeepSeek proof remains disclosed as not tested.
- **RF-003:** Addressed in `Merge risk`: the auth-provider boundary is called out for maintainer review, with the explicit-model-only gate and in-process metadata-preserving authorization described.
- **RF-004:** Addressed by replacing test-only proof with redacted live spawn result plus child session selected-model evidence.
- **RF-005:** Disclosed: this update supplies contributor proof and leaves final auth-provider boundary acceptance to maintainers.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit `sessions_spawn.model` forwards resolved provider/model to the child launch; in-process override authorization preserves scoped client metadata; no-explicit-model spawns do not become explicit provider/model overrides.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests assert the exact gateway `agent` request shape at the seam that starts the child run, avoiding live-provider flakiness while proving the routing data the gateway handler consumes.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** auth-provider, security-boundary, session-state, message-delivery, availability, compatibility, automation.
- **Risk explanation:** The change touches provider/model routing and gateway authorization for sub-agent launches. The public contract risk is limited to the existing `sessions_spawn.model` parameter; related open PRs were scanned by the daily-fix flow and treated as advisory background, not blockers. Message delivery/session fields are only moved in formatting around the same `agent` request object, not semantically changed.
- **Why acceptable:** Forwarding is gated on an explicit `sessions_spawn.model` value, so automatic/default model resolution is not converted into a gateway override. In-process authorization merges `allowModelOverride` into the scoped client instead of forcing a synthetic admin client, preserving plugin/run metadata. Regression tests cover explicit and no-explicit-model paths.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `spawnSubagentDirect` resolved `params.model` and persisted the resolved provider/model into the child session, but the subsequent gateway `agent` call that starts the child run did not include that resolved `provider`/`model`; the gateway handler can only honor explicit model routing from request fields it receives.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch changes the source-of-truth launch request before the child model is selected, rather than relying on status metadata or downstream fallback after the wrong route is chosen.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: source-level regression tests cover the request shape, and local live gateway proof shows an explicit provider/model selected by the spawned child run. The only remaining proof gap is the reporter's exact QQ/Qwen/DeepSeek setup.
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused production diff plus regression tests. The timeout field appears in the patch only because the `agent` request object was wrapped to pass authorization options; the value and timeout behavior are unchanged. No guard/catch/default workaround or lockfile change.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Owner boundary is `spawnSubagentDirect` constructing the child gateway `agent` request and `dispatchGatewayMethodInProcessRaw` selecting the gateway client metadata. This changes routing data before gateway model selection; it does not alter mirrored session status alone, public schema, or downstream provider fallback policy.
